description: Complete index of all NOSTR Implementation Possibilities (NIPs)

categories.core: Fundamental protocol definitions, event structure, relay communication
categories.identity: Key management, user identity, authentication, signing
categories.social: Social networking features: follows, reactions, reposts, threads
categories.messaging: Direct messages, group chats, encrypted communication
categories.media: File storage, images, video, audio, torrents
categories.applications: Application-specific protocols, DVMs, marketplace, calendar, wiki
categories.relay-management: Relay configuration, discovery, monitoring, access control
categories.security: Encryption, proof of work, content moderation, reporting

nips.title: Basic protocol flow description
nips.description: Defines the fundamental event structure (id, pubkey, created_at, kind, tags, content, sig), client-relay communication via WebSocket, filters, subscription model, and basic event kinds 0 and 1.

nips.title: Follow List
nips.description: Defines kind 3 events for maintaining a public list of followed pubkeys with optional relay URLs for each contact.

nips.title: OpenTimestamps Attestations for Events
nips.description: Defines kind 1040 events for creating OpenTimestamps attestations that prove the existence of a nostr event at a specific point in time.

nips.title: Encrypted Direct Message
nips.description: DEPRECATED: Defines kind 4 encrypted direct messages using shared ECDH secret and AES-256-CBC. Superseded by NIP-17 for better privacy.

nips.title: Mapping Nostr keys to DNS-based internet identifiers
nips.description: Maps nostr public keys to DNS-based internet identifiers (user@domain.com) via a well-known URL, enabling human-readable identity verification.

nips.title: Basic key derivation from mnemonic seed phrase
nips.description: Specifies how to derive nostr keys from BIP-39 mnemonic seed phrases using BIP-32 derivation path m/44'/1237'/0'/0/0.

nips.title: window.nostr capability for web browsers
nips.description: Defines a window.nostr JavaScript object that browser extensions can inject to provide key management and event signing capabilities.

nips.title: Handling Mentions
nips.description: DEPRECATED: Original mention handling via positional event tag references. Superseded by NIP-27 which uses nostr: URI scheme references.

nips.title: Event Deletion Request
nips.description: Defines kind 5 events as deletion requests, allowing users to signal that previously published events should be removed by relays.

nips.title: Text Notes and Threads
nips.description: Describes conventions for threading text notes using e and p tags, defining root, reply, and mention markers for kind 1 events.

nips.title: Relay Information Document
nips.description: Defines a JSON document served by relays at their WebSocket URL via HTTP, providing relay metadata such as name, description, supported NIPs, and limitations.

nips.title: Proof of Work
nips.description: Defines a proof-of-work mechanism for nostr events by requiring leading zero bits in the event id hash, used for spam prevention.

nips.title: Subject tag in text events
nips.description: Defines a 'subject' tag for kind 1 text events, enabling email-like subject lines for text notes.

nips.title: Nostr Marketplace
nips.description: Defines event kinds for decentralized marketplaces including stalls (30017), products (30018), UI/UX settings (30019), auctions (30020), bids (1021), and bid confirmations (1022).

nips.title: Private Direct Messages
nips.description: Defines private direct messages using kind 14 events wrapped with NIP-59 gift wrap for metadata protection. Replaces NIP-04.

nips.title: Reposts
nips.description: Defines kind 6 for reposting kind 1 text notes and kind 16 for generic reposts of any event kind.

nips.title: bech32-encoded entities
nips.description: Defines bech32-encoded strings for nostr entities: npub, nsec, note for basic types and nprofile, nevent, nrelay, naddr for shareable identifiers with relay hints.

nips.title: nostr: URI scheme
nips.description: Defines the nostr: URI scheme for referencing nostr entities using NIP-19 encoded strings (e.g., nostr:npub1..., nostr:nevent1...).

nips.title: Comment
nips.description: Defines kind 1400 for comments that can be attached to any event kind, web URLs, or other external content, enabling universal commenting.

nips.title: Long-form Content
nips.description: Defines kind 30023 for long-form text content (articles/blog posts) as parameterized replaceable events, with kind 30024 for drafts.

nips.title: Extra metadata fields and tags
nips.description: Defines additional optional metadata fields for kind 0 profiles and extra tags used across events, such as client tags and proxy tags.

nips.title: Reactions
nips.description: Defines kind 7 reaction events allowing users to react to other events with emoji or custom content. Kind 17 for website reactions.

nips.title: Delegated Event Signing
nips.description: UNRECOMMENDED: Defines a delegation mechanism allowing one keypair to authorize another to sign events on its behalf using delegation tags.

nips.title: Text Note References
nips.description: Defines how to reference other events and profiles within text note content using nostr: URI scheme (nostr:nevent1..., nostr:npub1...).

nips.title: Public Chat
nips.description: Defines public chat channels with kinds 40 (create), 41 (metadata), 42 (message), 43 (hide message), and 44 (mute user).

nips.title: Relay-based Groups
nips.description: Defines relay-based group functionality with group control events (kinds 9000-9030) and group metadata events (kinds 39000-39009).

nips.title: Custom Emoji
nips.description: Defines custom emoji using emoji tags with shortcodes and image URLs, referenceable in event content and reactions.

nips.title: Dealing with Unknown Events
nips.description: Describes how clients and relays should handle events with unknown kinds, including alt tag for human-readable descriptions.

nips.title: Labeling
nips.description: Defines kind 1985 label events for categorizing and labeling content with namespaced labels and label targets.

nips.title: git stuff
nips.description: Defines event kinds for Git-related collaboration: repository announcements (30617), patches (1617), pull requests (1618/1619), issues (1621), and status events (1630-1633).

nips.title: Torrents
nips.description: Defines kind 2003 for torrent metadata events and kind 2004 for torrent comments, enabling decentralized torrent indexing.

nips.title: Sensitive Content
nips.description: Defines a content-warning tag for events containing sensitive or NSFW content, enabling clients to hide or blur such content.

nips.title: Draft Events
nips.description: Defines kind 31234 for storing draft events privately, allowing clients to save work-in-progress events before publishing.

nips.title: User Statuses
nips.description: Defines kind 30315 for user status updates, allowing users to share what they are currently doing, listening to, or their general status.

nips.title: External Identities in Profiles
nips.description: Defines kind 10011 for linking external identity proofs (GitHub, Twitter, etc.) to nostr profiles for cross-platform verification.

nips.title: Expiration Timestamp
nips.description: Defines an 'expiration' tag that specifies a UNIX timestamp after which the event should be considered expired and may be deleted by relays.

nips.title: Authentication of clients to relays
nips.description: Defines the AUTH message flow for clients to authenticate to relays using kind 22242 events, enabling access-controlled relays.

nips.title: Relay Access Metadata and Requests
nips.description: Defines relay access control events including add user (8000), remove user (8001), join request (28934), invite request (28935), leave request (28936), and membership lists (13534).

nips.title: Encrypted Payloads (Versioned)
nips.description: Defines a versioned encryption scheme using NIP-44 conversation keys for encrypting event payloads, replacing NIP-04 encryption.

nips.title: Counting results
nips.description: Defines the COUNT message type for requesting event counts from relays matching specific filters, without downloading all events.

nips.title: Nostr Remote Signing
nips.description: Defines Nostr Connect protocol using kind 24133 events for remote signing, allowing keys to be held on a separate device or service.

nips.title: Nostr Wallet Connect
nips.description: Defines a protocol for connecting wallets using kind 13194 (wallet info), 23194 (wallet request), and 23195 (wallet response) for Lightning payments.

nips.title: Proxy Tags
nips.description: Defines proxy tags for events that are bridged from other protocols (ActivityPub, AT Protocol, etc.) to nostr.

nips.title: Private Key Encryption
nips.description: Defines a standard for encrypting nostr private keys with a password using scrypt key derivation and XChaCha20-Poly1305, producing ncryptsec bech32 strings.

nips.title: Search Capability
nips.description: Defines a search filter field for REQ messages, enabling full-text search capabilities on supporting relays.

nips.title: Lists
nips.description: Defines various list types: mute (10000), pin (10001), bookmarks (10003), communities (10004), follow sets (30000), relay sets (30002), bookmark sets (30003), curation sets (30004), emoji sets (30030), and more.

nips.title: Calendar Events
nips.description: Defines calendar event kinds: date-based (31922), time-based (31923), calendar collections (31924), and RSVPs (31925).

nips.title: Live Activities
nips.description: Defines live event streaming with kind 30311 (live event), 30312 (interactive room), 30313 (conference event), 10312 (room presence), and 1311 (live chat message).

nips.title: Wiki
nips.description: Defines kind 30818 for wiki articles and kind 30819 for redirects, enabling collaborative wiki content on nostr. Kind 818 for merge requests.

nips.title: Android Signer Application
nips.description: Defines a protocol for Android signer applications to handle key management and event signing via intents, similar to NIP-07 for web.

nips.title: Reporting
nips.description: Defines kind 1984 for reporting events or users for content moderation purposes with categorized report types.

nips.title: Lightning Zaps
nips.description: Defines Lightning Network zaps using kind 9734 (zap request) and kind 9735 (zap receipt) for sending sats to nostr users and events.

nips.title: Badges
nips.description: Defines badge system with kind 8 (badge award), kind 30008 (profile badges), and kind 30009 (badge definition) for recognizing user achievements.

nips.title: Gift Wrap
nips.description: Defines kind 13 (seal) and kind 1059 (gift wrap) for wrapping events in multiple encryption layers to protect metadata and content.

nips.title: Pubkey Static Websites
nips.description: Defines static website hosting via nostr using kind 15128 (root nsite manifest), kind 35128 (named nsite manifest), with kind 34128 deprecated.

nips.title: Cashu Wallet
nips.description: Defines Cashu ecash wallet events: kind 17375 (wallet event), kind 7374 (reserved tokens), kind 7375 (tokens), and kind 7376 (wallet history).

nips.title: Nutzaps
nips.description: Defines kind 9321 nutzap events and kind 10019 mint recommendation events for sending Cashu ecash tokens as zaps.

nips.title: Request to Vanish
nips.description: Defines kind 62 for requesting complete removal of all user data from relays, a stronger version of event deletion.

nips.title: Chess (PGN)
nips.description: Defines kind 64 for chess games using PGN (Portable Game Notation) format over nostr.

nips.title: Relay List Metadata
nips.description: Defines kind 10002 for publishing a user's preferred relay list with read/write designations, enabling relay discovery.

nips.title: Relay Discovery and Liveness Monitoring
nips.description: Defines kind 10166 (relay monitor announcement) and kind 30166 (relay discovery) for monitoring relay availability and metadata.

nips.title: Picture-first feeds
nips.description: Defines kind 20 for picture events optimized for image-first feeds, similar to Instagram-style content.

nips.title: Peer-to-peer Order events
nips.description: Defines kind 38383 for peer-to-peer trading order events, enabling decentralized order books.

nips.title: Protected Events
nips.description: Defines a '-' tag to mark events as protected, meaning relays should only accept them from the event author, preventing replay attacks.

nips.title: Video Events
nips.description: Defines kinds 21 (video event), 22 (short-form portrait video), 34235 (addressable video), and 34236 (addressable short video) for video content.

nips.title: Moderated Communities
nips.description: Defines kind 34550 for community definitions and kind 4550 for community post approvals, enabling moderated community spaces.

nips.title: External Content IDs
nips.description: Defines i-tags for referencing external content by identifiers such as URLs, ISBNs, DOIs, hashtags, geohashes, and podcast GUIDs.

nips.title: Zap Goals
nips.description: Defines kind 9041 for zap goal events, enabling fundraising targets that track zap progress toward a goal amount.

nips.title: Negentropy Syncing
nips.description: Defines the negentropy protocol for efficient set reconciliation between clients and relays, enabling fast event syncing.

nips.title: Application-specific data
nips.description: Defines kind 30078 for storing arbitrary application-specific data as parameterized replaceable events.

nips.title: Threads
nips.description: Defines kind 11 for thread events, providing a dedicated event kind for threaded discussions.

nips.title: Highlights
nips.description: Defines kind 9802 for highlight events, allowing users to save and share highlighted excerpts from long-form content.

nips.title: Trusted Assertions
nips.description: Defines trusted assertion events: kind 30382 (user), kind 30383 (event), and kind 30384 (addressable) for making verifiable claims.

nips.title: Relay Management API
nips.description: Defines an API for relay management operations, enabling remote administration of relay settings and configurations.

nips.title: Ecash Mint Discoverability
nips.description: Defines kind 38172 for Cashu mint announcements and kind 38173 for Fedimint announcements, enabling mint discovery on nostr.

nips.title: Polls
nips.description: Defines kind 1068 for poll events and kind 1018 for poll responses, enabling decentralized polling on nostr.

nips.title: Recommended Application Handlers
nips.description: Defines kind 31989 (handler recommendation) and kind 31990 (handler information) for recommending applications that can handle specific event kinds.

nips.title: Data Vending Machines
nips.description: Defines a marketplace for computational services: job requests (kinds 5000-5999), job results (kinds 6000-6999), and job feedback (kind 7000).

nips.title: Media Attachments
nips.description: Defines imeta tags for attaching media metadata (URL, MIME type, dimensions, hash) to events.

nips.title: File Metadata
nips.description: Defines kind 1063 for file metadata events describing files stored externally, including hash, URL, MIME type, and size.

nips.title: HTTP File Storage Integration
nips.description: UNRECOMMENDED: Defines an HTTP API for uploading files to nostr-aware file storage servers with NIP-98 authentication.

nips.title: HTTP Auth
nips.description: Defines kind 27235 for HTTP authentication using nostr events as bearer tokens for authenticating to HTTP servers.

nips.title: Classified Listings
nips.description: Defines kind 30402 for classified listings and kind 30403 for draft listings, enabling Craigslist-style classified ads on nostr.

nips.title: Voice Messages
nips.description: Defines kind 1222 for voice messages and kind 1244 for voice message comments.

nips.title: Public Messages
nips.description: Defines kind 24 for public messages, providing a distinct event kind for public messaging.

nips.title: Web Bookmarks
nips.description: Defines kind 39701 for web bookmark events, enabling users to save and share web bookmarks on nostr.

nips.title: Blossom
nips.description: Defines the Blossom protocol for blob storage on media servers with kind 10063 (user server list) and kind 24242 (blob events).

nips.title: Nostr BLE Communications Protocol
nips.description: Defines a Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) transport protocol for nostr event exchange between nearby devices.

nips.title: Code Snippets
nips.description: Defines kind 1337 for sharing code snippets on nostr with syntax highlighting metadata.

nips.title: Chats
nips.description: Defines kind 9 for chat message events within relay-based groups and other chat contexts.

nips.title: E2EE Messaging using MLS Protocol
nips.description: UNRECOMMENDED: Defines end-to-end encrypted group messaging using the MLS (Messaging Layer Security) protocol over nostr.

event_kinds_summary.regular: Events with kinds 1, 2, 4-44, 1000-9999 are regular events stored by relays
event_kinds_summary.replaceable: Events with kinds 0, 3, 10000-19999 are replaceable (only latest kept per pubkey+kind)
event_kinds_summary.ephemeral: Events with kinds 20000-29999 are ephemeral (not stored)
event_kinds_summary.parameterized_replaceable: Events with kinds 30000-39999 are parameterized replaceable (latest kept per pubkey+kind+d-tag)
